truths.truth: Filamentous bacteria extend outside sludge floc and contribute to bulking.
Zoogleal bacteria form compact floc that settles well.
Free-swimming bacteria are commonly associated with very young sludge.
Stalked ciliates usually indicate a stable activated sludge process.
Rotifers are commonly found in older sludge and low F/M conditions.
Nematodes usually indicate very old sludge.
Low dissolved oxygen promotes filamentous growth.
Nitrifying bacteria are sensitive to low dissolved oxygen and low alkalinity.
